title: Docker Containerization Basics
description: Learn standard commands for running containers, listing containers, building images, stopping workloads, and running terminal commands inside containers.

tasks.prompt: Run an 'nginx' container in detached mode (-d), mapping port '80' of the host to port '80' of the container.
tasks.hint: Use docker run with detached (-d) and port (-p) flags: 'docker run -d -p 80:80 nginx'
tasks.explanation: 'docker run' downloads the image if needed and spins it up. '-d' runs it in the background, and '-p 80:80' maps port 80.
'docker ps' lists container IDs, images, commands, creation time, port mappings, and running names.

tasks.prompt: Build a Docker image tagged as 'app:v1' using the Dockerfile in the current directory.
tasks.hint: Use tag flag (-t) and specify path as dot (.): 'docker build -t app:v1 .'
tasks.explanation: 'docker build' reads the local Dockerfile (indicated by '.') to build a container image, tagging it with '-t'.

tasks.prompt: Stop a running container with ID 'c123'.
tasks.hint: Use the stop command: 'docker stop c123'
tasks.explanation: 'docker stop' sends a SIGTERM signal to the main process inside the container, stopping it gracefully.

tasks.prompt: Run an interactive shell command line inside the container 'c123' using the 'bash' shell.
tasks.hint: Use exec with interactive tty flags (-it) and bash: 'docker exec -it c123 bash'
tasks.explanation: 'docker exec' spawns a new process inside a running container. '-it' binds the host terminal input/output.
